## DoorDash Declines 5.8% After Wells Fargo Holds Equal-Weight Rather Than Upgrading

A 5.8% drop on a rating-maintain (not a downgrade) indicates DASH was overpositioned into this analyst event; PM should assess whether the positioning reset creates a more attractive entry or whether weak fundamental expectations are the underlying issue Wells Fargo declined to upgrade through.

- DASH shares fell 5.8%, with the move directly attributed to Wells Fargo maintaining its Equal-Weight rating.
- The selloff reflects an expectation reset — the market had apparently positioned for an upgrade, and a rating-hold disappointed those expectations.
- No fundamental catalyst, earnings event, or guidance change was cited; this is entirely a sentiment and positioning unwind.
- No broader theme tag assigned by the classifier, confirming this is a company-specific technicals story.
